campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council met on March 24, 2026, to honor K9 Lucas, a police dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward on several items including an updated beekeeping ordinance, a study about potentially creating homeless services in the West Valley area, and a discussion about transit-oriented development near future transit zones.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial - police department's first working dog
Beekeeping ordinance updated and adopted
West Valley Homeless Services feasibility study and regional task force
Senate Bill 79 transit-oriented development discussion with some council recusals

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a special meeting to review and approve the work plans for five different city commissions for the next fiscal year. The commissions covered areas like preserving local history, running the library and art programs, managing parks, improving traffic safety, and overseeing development planning. The council also discussed a legal case in a private session.
keyTopics: Approved work priorities for Heritage Preservation Commission
Library and Public Art Commission programs and goals
Parks and Recreation Commission plans for next year
Traffic Safety Commission initiatives
Planning Commission development oversight priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving previous meeting minutes and the treasurer's report. The council also adopted policies on weed removal, affordable housing unit transfers, and city procedures, while approving two new flag raisings to celebrate Pride and Juneteenth.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion report
Affordable housing (BMR) unit transfer rules
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
New city council procedures and norms adopted
Housing element implementation timeline discussed

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve various administrative records and review the city's financial health and future plans. Key items included approving meeting minutes, reviewing the city's January finances, accepting an annual housing and planning report, and moving forward with the Solana Project development.
keyTopics: Approval of Solana Project final development map (Tract 10657)
January 2026 monthly financial and investment report review
Annual General Plan and Housing Element progress update
2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan approval
Meeting procedures and minute-taking format changes

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for April 7 and April 14, 2026. They reviewed which new items to add to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked whether any documents needed special notice requirements. The council also reviewed the public record from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized April 7 meeting agenda and items
Reviewed draft agenda for April 14 meeting
Set time slots for specific agenda items
Checked waiver requirements for documents
Reviewed public records from March 19-26

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for residents to speak during public comment. The council also reviewed reports from various city committees and discussed upcoming meetings for different city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Schedules for city boards and commissions meetings
Recreation Division contact information for questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund housing and community development projects in the city. The commission approved previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations applying for these grants to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal housing grants (CDBG funding) being distributed to local organizations
Community development projects seeking city funding
Housing and human services commission operations and meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses in Cupertino grow and thrive. They discussed offering a special amnesty program to help businesses get their licenses in order, promoting shopping at local stores through summer events, and partnering with private companies to sponsor city events and provide incentives for retail shops and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get compliant without penalties
Shopping center outreach - encouraging residents to shop local
Summer of Celebrations campaign - promoting local businesses through community events
Public-private partnerships for event sponsorships - businesses helping fund city events
Economic incentives for small businesses and startups - making it easier for new companies to open in Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026 to review matters related to the city's historic buildings and sites. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and could submit written comments by email before the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline (12 p.m. day of meeting)
Review of historic buildings and landmarks in Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative business, including approving meeting minutes and setting the calendar for upcoming meetings. The council also approved travel for Mayor Montano to attend a conference and confirmed a new representative to serve on the Economic Development board.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes
Mayor Montano attending WELL 2026 Annual conference
New School District representative appointed to Economic Development board
Reviewed upcoming agenda items requested by council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine items including officially recognizing March as Women's History Month. The council approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city buildings as emergency shelters during disasters, and agreed to spend about $93,400 per year for the next three years on email security software (Mimecast) to protect the city's computer systems.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster shelter agreement with city facilities
Email security software contract ($280,000+ over 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
